titleStmt.title: Letter from A R Barlas to Sir Matthew Nathan, 28 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Alexander Richard Barlas

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Alexander Richard Barlas (1862-) the Secretary for the Local Government Board to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) who was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising. In the letter Barlas is forwarding a complaint, about the delay of work caused by the Crown Solicitor of Cavan Louis C.P. Smith (1867-1923), on behalf of the Board. Barlas explains that the board are not happy with the delays caused by the Crown Solicitor.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/4061

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Local Government Board, Dublin 28th. February, 1916. Immediate
Mr Smith - Crown Solicitor.
The Under Secretary would be
obliged for your observations as to B. It is
most desirable that the audit of these
claims should be completed without further
delay. J.S.T. 29.2.16 Sir, With reference to Mr. Taylor's minute of the 21st instant relative to
the Audit of the Claims in respect of Prosecutors and Witnesses Expenses in
the case of the County of Cavan for the Half-Years ended 31st March, 1914,
30th September, 1914, 31st March, 1915 and 30th September, 1915, I am
directed by the Local Government Board for Ireland to state that they
forwarded the Minute in question to their Auditor, Mr. Newell, who made the
following observations thereon:- "Some three weeks ago I wrote to the Crown Solicitor forwarding certain
"queries in respect of this claim and asking for an early reply but he
"has not answered yet. I wrote again a couple of days ago pressing for
"a reply as the matter was urgent and expect a reply this week. I shall
"certify the Claim without delay when I hear from the Crown Solicitor. "I have frequently spoken to the Accountant in County Cavan about
"his delay in sending on the Claims but without result. In this
"instance he has allowed four half years to accumulate which is very
"inconvenient. I should be obliged if the Board would kindly inform him
"that these claims must be promptly forwarded in future." The Board desire me to point out as regards the observations of Mr.
Newell marked A that they are in communication with the County Council in
the matter. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, A R Barlas
Secretary. The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle.
